title: requests — third-party subprocessors
description: This module does NOT directly integrate with any third-party subprocessor. Every external touchpoint (mail delivery for notifications, database, cache) is owned by a UPSTREAM module (`notifications` for mail transports, `foundation` for Redis/DB) whose own subprocessors.json documents its providers. This file exists for uniform blueprint shape and to make the empty state explicit — auditors verifying the DPA registry get a clean 'no direct integrations' signal.

indirectSubprocessors.description: Mail transport for AccessRequestSubmittedNotification, AccessRequestApprovedNotification, AccessRequestRejectedNotification, AccessRequestExpiredNotification, AccessRequestReminderNotification. Actual transport per tenant is one of SendGrid / SES / Postmark / Mailgun — see `modules/notifications/subprocessors.json` for the DPA-registered list.
Postgres for the underlying approval_instances + access_grants storage; Redis for cache keys + queue backing. Standard shared infrastructure.

operational_notes: Because there is no direct integration, no per-module DPA action is required if a mail transport is added or removed. The notifications module owns those transitions.
If a future version of this module introduces a dedicated subprocessor (e.g. Twilio for SMS access-request escalation), the DPA + region + region metadata will land here.
